name: samba-common-bin
title: samba-common-bin
summary: Samba common files used by both the server and the client
description:
Samba common files used by both the server and the client
Samba is an implementation of the SMB/CIFS protocol for Unix systems,
providing support for cross-platform file sharing with Microsoft Windows, OS X,
and other Unix systems.  Samba can also function as a domain controller
or member server in Active Directory or NT4-style domains.
.
This package contains the common files that are used by both the server
(provided in the samba package) and the client (provided in the smbclient
package).

commands.name: dbwrap_tool
commands.description: low level TDB/CTDB manipulation tool using the dbwrap interface

arguments.description: This will redirect debug output to STDOUT. By default all clients are logging to STDERR.
arguments.raw: --debug-stdout  This will redirect debug output to STDOUT. By default all clients are logging to STDERR.

arguments.description: Enable talloc leak reporting on exit.
arguments.raw: --leak-report  Enable talloc leak reporting on exit.

arguments.description: Enable full talloc leak reporting on exit.
arguments.raw: --leak-report-full  Enable full talloc leak reporting on exit.

arguments.description: Open the database as a non-persistent database.
arguments.raw: --non-persistent  Open the database as a non-persistent database.

arguments.description: Open the database as a persistent database.
arguments.raw: --persistent  Open the database as a persistent database.

commands.name: net
commands.description: Tool for administration of Samba and remote CIFS servers.

commands.name: nmblookup
commands.description: NetBIOS over TCP/IP client used to lookup NetBIOS names

commands.name: samba-regedit
commands.description: ncurses based tool to manage the Samba registry

commands.name: smbcontrol
commands.description: send messages to smbd, nmbd or winbindd processes

commands.name: testparm
commands.description: check an smb.conf configuration file for internal correctness

commands.name: winexe
commands.description: Winexe is a Remote Windows-command executor

command_examples: uint32: dbwrap_tool --persistent winbindd_idmap.tdb store "USER HWM" uint32 214  hex:  db-
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dbwrap_tool because Samba common files used by both the server and the client.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dbwrap_tool as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dbwrap_tool  [-?|--help]  [--usage]  [--persistent]  [--non-persistent] [-d|--debuglevel=DEBU-` as documented upstream for dbwrap_tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dbwrap_tool --persistent winbindd_idmap.tdb listkeys` as documented upstream for dbwrap_tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dbwrap_tool --persistent winbindd_idmap.tdb fetch "USER HWM" uint32` as documented upstream for dbwrap_tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dbwrap_tool --persistent winbindd_idmap.tdb remove "USER HWM"` as documented upstream for dbwrap_tool.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dbwrap_tool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dbwrap_tool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: dbwrap_tool - low level TDB/CTDB manipulation tool using the dbwrap interface
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install samba. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dbwrap_tool  [-?|--help]  [--usage]  [--persistent]  [--non-persistent] [-d|--debuglevel=DEBU-`. Further invocations from upstream: `dbwrap_tool --persistent winbindd_idmap.tdb listkeys`; `dbwrap_tool --persistent winbindd_idmap.tdb fetch "USER HWM" uint32`. Samba common files used by both the server and the client.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Samba common files used by both the server and the client.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dbwrap_tool - low level TDB/CTDB manipulation tool using the dbwrap interface.
